firm.notes: Firm posts performance highlights on website and LinkedIn, and uses testimonials in pitch decks.

scenario.description: The firm wants to publish a website page and a LinkedIn post that includes (1) a client testimonial, (2) a claim 'top quartile returns', and (3) a 1-year performance chart. The content will be used for prospecting.

sources.title: Marketing Rule, general anti-fraud standard
sources.excerpt: An advertisement may not include any untrue statement of a material fact, or omit a material fact necessary to make the statement not misleading.

sources.title: Testimonials and endorsements disclosure concept
sources.excerpt: Certain testimonials and endorsements are permitted, subject to conditions including disclosures about compensation, conflicts of interest, and whether the person is a client.

sources.title: Performance advertising, fair and balanced presentation concept
sources.excerpt: Performance results should be presented in a manner that is fair and balanced and not materially misleading in light of the circumstances.

sources.title: Substantiation expectation concept
sources.excerpt: The adviser should have a reasonable basis for believing it will be able to substantiate material statements of fact in an advertisement upon demand.
